items.summary: Community Summit
items.description: 9am - 9:30am - Welcome John Picozzi, AmyJune Hineline Welcome to the Community Summit we will kick off with an overview of our schedule, important information for the day, and a fun ice breaker to get everyone energized.

items.summary: Government Summit
items.description: Thank you for joining us for the Government Summit at DrupalCon Atlanta 2025! Here you will find the detailed schedule for the day. Please note schedule details are subject to change, and additional content will be added as it becomes available.

items.summary: Healthcare Summit
items.description: The Healthcare Summit

items.summary: Higher Education Summit
items.description: 9:00 - 9:05 Opening Remarks 9:05 - 9:25 Lightning Talk #1: From Buzzword to Impact: Harnessing AI for Inclusive Innovation by Tina Miller 9:25 - 9:45 Table Breakout #1 : AI

items.description: 9:00am-9:15am: Welcome Stephen: Welcome and overview 9:30am-11:30am: Fireside Chats - 9:30am: Cristina and Pamela: The Future of DrupalCMS - 10:15am: Emma: User Experience in DrupalCMS - 11:15: Tim Lehnen: Drupal for Nonprofits: nuances to bring back to your org

items.description: Attendance requires a training session ticket purchase ($500).

items.description: Attendance requires a training session ticket purchase ($500).

items.description: There's only one better Drupal-feeling than seeing things you have worked on being committed into Drupal; helping and assisting someone else to do the same! Thursday is mentored contribution day and you can be a key part of it!

items.description: Meeting space for organized local camps, associations, or communities with designated ambassadors. Creating a welcoming and functional space for fostering a sense of community and collaboration.

items.summary: Quiet Room
items.location: Location Executive Conference Room 219 - Hotel 2nd Floor
items.description: This room is available for mediation and reflection. It is in Executive Conference Room 219, located on the 2nd Floor of the Hyatt Hotel.

items.summary: REGISTRATION
Scholarship Breakfast (invite only)
Coffee Break in Expo Hall
4 Big Ideas in UX and Content Strategy
items.description: What drives investment in your website? For some organizations, a small group of stakeholders sets the priorities. Others conduct user research, or a new marketing director may shake things up with fresh ideas.

items.summary: Documentation Initiatives for Starshot and Drupal.org
items.description: High-quality documentation is crucial for Drupal CMS's wider adoption, community growth, and overall success. As part of the Drupal Starshot initiative, we're focusing on improving our documentation and creating a highly curated, best-in class Drupal CMS User Guide among other things.

items.summary: Don't put it all on the homepage!
items.description: So many websites suffer from content bloat, especially the all-important homepage, as they try to meet the needs of a wide-ranging audience (especially internal staff and leadership).

items.summary: First-Time Contributor Workshop
items.description: Do you want to contribute but are not sure how? Check out the First Time Contributor Pathway for a list of recommended sessions at DrupalCon Atlanta to help you figure out how to use your particular skills to contribute to the Drupal project!

items.summary: IXP-Fellowship: Using Contribution Credits to encourage organizations to hire new Drupal talent
items.description: The IXP-Fellowship, a Drupal community initiative for onboarding inexperienced developers has its initial goal of using Drupal's contribution credit system as an incentive for Drupal organizations to hire new and inexperienced Drupal developers.

items.summary: Mapping Success: Building Effective Product Roadmaps for Drupal Projects
items.description: In today’s fast-paced digital landscape, effective product roadmaps are essential for aligning stakeholders, setting priorities, and guiding the successful execution of Drupal projects.

items.summary: Meet the AI Automators that power everything in Drupal CMS - CKEditor, AI Agents, no code required!
items.description: Meet the AI Automators that power all the AI features you’ve seen in Drupal CMS. They support the content editor experience through CKEditor and other Content widgets, underpin the alt text generation and even help you create your own Agents with no code.

items.summary: Recipes: It's About Time!
items.description: One of the key elements of the Starshot Initiative is the rapidly evolving system for Recipes.

items.summary: Tag! You're it. Digital "freeze" tag with GTM.
items.description: In today’s digital landscape, effective tag management is essential for improving website performance and maintaining privacy compliance.

items.summary: The Future of Drupal Theming: AI, Experience Builder, and Beyond
items.description: Drupal’s theming layer has grown by leaps and bounds over the past several years. We’ve seen new Twig filters, theme generation tools, and the introduction of Single Directory Components.

items.summary: Leveraging gitlab CI for contributions
items.description: In the fast-paced world of Drupal development, efficient and reliable workflows are crucial for successful contributions and project management.

items.summary: Coffee Break in Expo Hall
Strategies for Inter-company Developer Communication
items.description: Differing priorities, methodologies, and backgrounds of developers from different companies can occasionally cause friction in communication and collaboration. But with an understanding of common pitfalls and strategies for avoiding them we can work toward better results.

items.summary: New Member + Local Drupal Community Meet Up prior to the Keynote
items.description: Let's get together to break the ice and meet new people before the Keynote.

items.summary: Quick-Start CI/CD Pipelines for Drupal on Acquia, Pantheon, Platform.sh, or Any Other Hosting
items.description: Are you looking for a fast and reliable way to automate Drupal deployments across various hosting platforms?

items.summary: KEYNOTE: Welcome & Aaron Winborn Award
KEYNOTE: Driesnote
Black in Drupal: Finding Community within Community
items.description: The panel titled "Black in Drupal: Finding Community within Community" aims to shed light on the unique experiences and challenges faced by individuals from underrepresented backgrounds within the Drupal community.

items.summary: DrupalCon Group Photo
Black in Drupal Lunch (guests must pre-register)
items.description: The Black in Drupal Roundtable at DrupalCon will serve as a forum to exchange ideas and information relevant to the experiences of being black in Drupal.

items.summary: Lunch & Expo Hall
The Marketeers Survival Guide to Drupal CMS, AI, and the End of Days
items.description: How will the new Drupal CMS increase opportunities to strengthen messaging across your organization and its audiences and what pitfalls will you have to avoid to realize their potential?

items.summary: Express Checkout in Drupal Commerce
items.description: Drupal Commerce features a multi-step checkout flow out of the box that stores can configure as needed. However, reducing it to a single page experience is difficult due to the data requirements of certain checkout panes.

items.summary: CKEditor 5 in Drupal 10: Unlocking Content Editor Productivity with the CKEditor 5 Plugin Pack
items.description: Discover how the CKEditor 5 Plugin Pack enhances the content editing experience in Drupal 10, streamlining workflows, improving formatting, and ensuring accessibility—all while providing premium features for free.

items.summary: Sovereign AI in production : Ensuring Privacy, Control, and Innovation (real world case)
items.description: In this lightning talk, we'll explore how the Dropsolid empowers organisations to adopt a Sovereign AI approach, ensuring data privacy and control while enabling innovation.

items.summary: AI is the new UI - how we are building AI into Drupal CMS
items.description: AI Agents have come to Drupal CMS!

items.summary: Community Working Group Roundtable
items.description: The Drupal Community Working Group are volunteers whose goal is to foster a friendly and welcoming environment for all who participate in the Drupal Project.

items.summary: Creating Composer aware modules with Drupal core's new Package Manager module
items.description: Package Manager, a new experimental module in Drupal Core, provides an API for interacting with Composer, which has been powering the Automatic Updates and Project Browser contrib modules for years.

items.summary: Design systems, the Drupal way
items.description: Design systems are a great way to streamline the web development process and ensure consistency across different applications by documenting shareable UI artefacts. However, implementing a design system in a Drupal theme is challenging.

items.summary: From Data to Insight: Crafting Custom GA4 Reports in Looker Studio for Website Success
items.description: In this hands-on workshop, you will be introduced to Looker Studio (formerly Google Data Studio), a powerful data visualization tool for building customizable, dynamic reports from Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and a wide range of other data sources.

items.summary: Mentor Orientation
items.description: There's only one better Drupal-feeling than seeing things you have worked on being committed into Drupal; helping and assisting someone else to do the same! Thursday is mentored contribution day and you can be a key part of it!

items.summary: No need to over-React! Navigating Experience Builder as a developer
items.description: Experience Builder (XB) will not only enable site builders to theme and build their entire website in the browser, it will also unlock content creation with unprecedented UX in Drupal.

items.summary: Security Team Panel
items.description: The security team will present topics from their work promoting a more secure Drupal project. The team will also talk about how they recruit new members, refinements to their procedures, and what you can do to support Drupal security.

items.summary: The Unspoken Algorithm: Neurodivergence, Identity, and Turning Exclusion into Inclusion
items.description: In the fast-paced world of tech entrepreneurship, we often celebrate the visible markers of success while overlooking the complex algorithms that drive innovation: our diverse minds and experiences.

items.summary: Unlocking Enterprise Agility: A Deep Dive into Governance and Multi-Experience Operations
items.description: Discover how Multi-Experience Operations (MEO) is set to revolutionize the way enterprises govern and scale digital experiences.

items.summary: Coffee Break in Expo Hall
"Survey Says!" — User Experience Research for Digital Platforms
items.description: During this session we'll dive into common UX Research activities and best practices when it comes to developing and executing research plans including Identifying & Recruiting Participants, 1:1 Interviews, A/B Testing, Behavioral Analytics, Card Sorting, Competitive Analysis, Heuristic Analy

items.summary: AI with Drupal - Using LLM technology is easy, but how do you actually build useful applications?
items.description: Using AI tools like ChatGPT from OpenAI is easy, and many already have experience with it. Common uses like creating or re-writing text are well integrated with Drupal.

items.summary: Digital Debris: Strategies for the Life and Death of PDFs
items.description: Our websites are full of PDFs meant to engage with audiences and customers, or to help provide services for government and public agencies, yet most organizations do not have a formal strategy for how their PDFs are created or distributed, or know what happens after they are published.

items.summary: Drupal Association Public Board Meeting
items.description: Join us for a public board meeting of the Drupal Association. Board members will talk about future programs, conduct regular business to support the Drupal Association's non-profit operations, and and answer community questions.

items.summary: Drupal Next-Gen Navigation: Enhanced Admin UI and better UX
items.description: Drupal’s administration UI is undergoing a significant transformation with the new Navigation and Top Bar. This change is set to deliver a more intuitive experience, offering enhanced usability that goes beyond simple navigation. Reshaping the entire layout of the admin interface.

items.summary: Drupal Recipes Initiative Update
items.description: The Recipes APIs were added to Drupal core in version 10.3 as experimental and laid the foundation for the Drupal CMS project. Since then, we have been adding and improving the code base, and learning new things along the way.

items.summary: Drupal Workspaces: Revolutionizing Content Staging and Workflows
items.description: Discover how the Drupal Workspace module can transform the way you manage content staging and workflows.

items.summary: Experience Builder is coming. Are you ready?
items.description: Join us to hear the latest news on the Experience Builder initiative from the initiative leaders.

items.summary: Mentoring Triage Issues
items.description: Thursday is mentored contribution day and you can be a key part of it! The mentored contribution days are one of the most amazing parts of big Drupal events - so many people working together to make Drupal even more wonderful.

items.summary: Mixing the Schema.org Blueprints module into a Drupal Recipe to bake a sweet content model
items.description: The Schema.org Blueprints module provides a new way to build a standardized and structured content architecture in Drupal. A Drupal Recipe is a new way to predefine and package configurations that add features to a new or existing Drupal implementation.

items.summary: Using Your Superpowers to Lead in a Male-Dominated Industry
items.description: Join us for an engaging discussion that delves into the challenges and opportunities of stepping into leadership roles within a male-dominated industry. We will focus on gender expectations and how we have navigated our own various paths to leadership.

items.summary: Working with the AI Agents in Drupal CMS - Create your own agents and AI powered migration
items.description: You’ve seen what AI Agents can do in Drupal CMS. You kept imagining all the other things agents could do. What if you could create your own Agents? What if this were so easy every module across the Drupal ecosystem could have their own agents and they all worked together in harmony?

items.summary: EXPO HALL NETWORKING
Accessible Slideshows - Button, button, who's got the button?
items.description: Arguably, the first rule of slideshows is not to have slideshows, but what do you do when the primary product owner insists?

items.summary: AI Won’t Steal Your Job—But It Will Make You (and Your Clients) Unstoppable
items.description: AI isn’t here to replace developers—it’s here to eliminate bottlenecks, crush inefficiencies, and make your agency more valuable than ever.

items.summary: Behind the Build: How We're Creating Drupal’s New Default Design System
items.description: Get a behind-the-scenes look into the creation of the new default design system for DrupalCMS.

items.summary: Building A Better Authoring Experience
items.description: The authors who enter content into our websites are a crucial part of our ecosystem who have often taken a back seat to other needs like splashy designs or improvements for developers.

items.summary: Building High-Performance Search Experiences with Solr and Drupal
items.description: Are you tired of slow and clunky search experiences on your Drupal site? Join this hands-on workshop to learn how to supercharge your search capabilities with Solr.

items.summary: Container-less Dev environments for Drupal development with Nix
items.description: DDev, Lando, and DrupalPod are all great ways to get a local development instance up and running, but they carry some overhead: they are all built on containers.

items.summary: Drupal CMS: The Exciting Parts
items.description: Part panel discussion, part game show, this session will feature Drupal experts debating which parts of Drupal CMS' are the one to be most excited about.

items.summary: If you Give a Member Platform a Cookie: Lessons Learned Creating a Drupal Product for Member Orgs
items.description: Membership organizations include clubs (running, French, or photography), professional associations (accounting or medical), civic groups (neighborhood, homeowners, or parent teacher associations), service clubs (Lions or Rotary), and many other nonprofits.

items.summary: Navigating Migration Challenges and Streamlining Content for Site Consolidation Projects
items.description: As organizations increasingly adopt site consolidation strategies to unify their brands and enhance their digital presence, content migration becomes a pivotal challenge.

items.summary: Ripple Makers Roundtable
items.description: Ripple Makers (Drupal Association Members) are invited to join us for a round table to chat with their fellow members and DA staff about the future of the program, the mission of the Drupal Association, and how they would like to see the Drupal Association evolve.

items.summary: Shaping the Future of Drupal: How Design Thinking + Collaboration is driving Experience Builder
items.description: The new Drupal Experience Builder is being shaped by a design thinking approach that emphasizes collaboration, iteration, and user-centered solutions.

items.summary: Working with Layout Paragraphs: an Easy-to-Use, Drag-and-Drop Content Editing module for Drupal
items.description: Layout Paragraphs is a widely used, contributed Drupal module that combines drag-and-drop editing, Drupal core's layout API, and the Paragraphs module to provide an accessible, easy-to-use authoring experience for building pages in Drupal.

items.summary: Mind Over Metrics: Behavioral Economics for Smarter Content Strategy 🧠
items.description: Are you tired of content strategies that feel more like guessing games than science? Behavioral economics may hold the key to why some messages inspire action, while others fade away.

items.summary: Birds of a Feather - Edgewood Room (Wednesday)
items.description: View the BoF schedule or submit your own: https://events.drupal.org/atlanta2025/bofs/all

items.summary: Birds of a Feather - Harris Room (Wednesday)
items.description: View the BoF schedule or submit your own: https://events.drupal.org/atlanta2025/bofs/all

items.description: View the BoF schedule or submit your own: https://events.drupal.org/atlanta2025/bofs/all

items.description: View the BoF schedule or submit your own: https://events.drupal.org/atlanta2025/bofs/all

items.summary: General Contribution
items.description: Generously Sponsored by ThirdAndGrove

items.summary: New Member + Local Drupal Community Meeting Space
items.description: Meeting space for organized local camps, associations, or communities with designated ambassadors. Creating a welcoming and functional space for fostering a sense of community and collaboration.

items.summary: Quiet Room
items.location: Location Executive Conference Room 219 - Hotel 2nd Floor
items.description: This room is available for mediation and reflection. It is in Executive Conference Room 219, located on the 2nd Floor of the Hyatt Hotel.

items.summary: REGISTRATION
Ripple Makers Breakfast (members only) (guest must pre-register)
items.description: A breakfast event for Ripple Makers (formerly known as Drupal Association members). We want to welcome and thank you for your support of the Drupal Association, and also hear what is important to you.

items.summary: Coffee Break in Expo Hall
Building web confidence through accessible, non-expert user-trainings
items.description: Poorly trained users often grow frustrated with their tools. This can ultimately lead to a breakdown of trust in web systems and the teams that maintain them.

items.summary: Drupal CMS now and beyond
items.description: With Drupal CMS 1.0 released in January, we're now well into development of the next version. So it's a great opportunity to discuss what we have planned next on the roadmap, provide updates on how we are tracking and briefly look back at the process so far.

items.summary: Drupal CMS: Golden standard for privacy and data protection
items.description: As the Drupal CMS strategy v1.0 describes, supporting the Open Web is our responsibility. Drupal's Open Web Manifesto clarifies, that the Open Web is strengthened by every individual’s right to choice, privacy, and security.

items.summary: From Figma to Function: Bridging Design and Development with Storybook & Drupal
items.description: This practical, hands-on workshop takes participants on a “storied” journey from UI design to development, starting from zero. You’ll hear perspectives from both a UI designer and a front-end developer and how to effectively collaborate across both disciplines.

items.summary: Leveraging Drupal SaaS to Power 400 Websites as Unique as Independent Bookstores
items.description: The American Booksellers Association (ABA), a national not-for-profit trade organization, works to ensure and protect the diversity, success, and profitability of independently owned book retailers.

items.summary: Overcoming the Cassandra Complex: How to Use a Redesign as a Trojan Horse for Organizational Change
items.description: My old boss used to say, “In higher ed, when 20 people want to do something, and one wants to do the opposite, that’s considered a tie.” This is a challenge many of us face: we see the benefits to changing our organization’s behaviors, but it may feel like the organization is unable or unwilling

items.summary: Paragraphs and Single Directory Components: A dynamic duo
items.description: There are so many factors at play when implementing Drupal, so many possible combinations of strategies, user preferences, and technical decisions that simply making a choice and sticking to it is a big win. We think we've found one such approach and want to share it with you!

items.summary: The future of Drupal core in the age of Drupal CMS
items.description: The introduction of Drupal CMS has created a buzz in the Drupal community, redefining how we think about building with Drupal. Its flexible, modern approach to development has opened up new possibilities for innovation.

items.summary: The Neurodivergency SuperPower - How Diverse Teams Function Better
items.description: In today's rapidly evolving work environment, successful teams embrace diversity in all its forms, especially neurodiversity.

items.summary: Coffee Break in Expo Hall
Beyond DrupalCMS - Revitalizing the Drupal Site Builder opportunities in the Post-Drupal 7 Era
items.description: The era of Drupal 7 is ending, leaving thousands of small-site owners searching for an affordable and easy way to continue their work without diving into complex development or costs they can't afford.

items.summary: New Member + Local Drupal Community Meet Up prior to the Keynote
items.description: Let's get together to break the ice and meet new people before the Keynote.

items.summary: KEYNOTE: Yamilee Toussaint
items.description: The Choreography of Innovation: Lessons from the Stage for Bold Thinking

items.summary: Lunch & Expo Hall
Women in Drupal Luncheon (guests must pre-register)
items.description: Lullabot celebrates, honors, and supports women who are working in Drupal and throughout the tech industry. Since Lullabot’s inception in 2006, we have fostered our company culture around the open-source value that everyone can make meaningful contributions and collaborate openly.

items.summary: From Manual to Magical: Our Pipeline Revamp
items.description: Pipeline automation using Platform.sh and its advantages for business acceleration. Speakers: Sam Rollin - CEO, Managing Director of Rollin agency Haylee Millar - Product Engineer, Platform.sh

items.summary: Building a Website in 5 Minutes with Drupal AI
items.description: Imagine creating a fully functional, AI-powered website in just five minutes—no coding required. With the Drupal AI module, this is now a reality.

items.summary: Generosity with Intelligence: AI for good
items.description: Learn how AI is used for good to matches you with volunteering or donation opportunities. A collaboration of Share Good and Four Kitchens.

items.summary: How Modern AI is Already Impacting the Future of Search
items.description: Discover how TraceLink, a leader in supply chain solutions, ambitiously transformed its website with an AI chatbot that integrates with Drupal's content ecosystem.

items.summary: Who Wants to Be a Drupal Millionaire?
items.description: Who Wants to Be a Drupal Millionaire? Format: A live game show with Drupal-related questions. Ask progressively harder Drupal trivia questions. Use polls, hand raises, or a Kahoot-style quiz for audience participation.

items.summary: Automate, Integrate, Innovate: AI-powered GitLab CI for Drupal module development
items.description: In this session, we will embark on a journey into the future of code review, where the integration of ChatGPT and GitLab CI revolutionizes the Drupal module development process.

items.summary: Developing the Product Management Practice in Government
items.description: This session will explore the development of the "product mindset" and product management practice within government digital services teams, specifically focusing on utilizing Drupal as a vehicle to drive innovation and continuously offer value to constituents.

items.summary: Discuss Site Templates and Marketplace (Driesnote Followup BoF)
items.description: In the Driesnote, project lead Dries Buytaert announced site templates for Drupal CMS to be built this year and marketplace of site templates to be discussed. Let's get together to discuss these ideas and possibilities and how you will be involved!

items.summary: Drupal for Real-Time Management of Android & iOS Apps
items.description: This session will showcase how Drupal can be integrated with Google Firebase to provide real-time management of content and configurations for iOS and Android applications in production environments.

items.summary: DrupalCon as a Game
items.description: Follow creation of a Drupal-focused Alternate Reality Game in the style of Pokemon Go you can play during DrupalCon. Game design, web APIs, and native mobile apps have been used to create a game for you to play and beta test during the conference.

items.summary: Finding Your Path to Drupal: How Three Unique Journeys Led to Meaningful Careers in Web Development
items.description: Drupal developers come from all walks of life, each bringing unique experiences and perspectives to their careers.

items.summary: First-Time Contribution Workshop
items.description: Do you want to contribute but are not sure how? Check out the First Time Contributor Pathway for a list of recommended sessions at DrupalCon Atlanta to help you figure out how to use your particular skills to contribute to the Drupal project!

items.summary: Leveling Up Content: Integrating Drupal with Godot for Game Development
items.description: What happens when the flexibility of Drupal meets the interactivity of Godot? In this session, we’ll dive into how you can harness the power of these two platforms to create dynamic, content-driven games.

items.summary: Site Building with Translations, Regionalization, and Layout Builder
items.description: This presentation aims to provide strategies for configuring and managing regionalization and translation in Drupal, with a focus on using tools like Platform.SH and Fastly CDN for geoIP detection.

items.summary: Supply Chain Security in Drupal and Composer
items.description: Everyone has heard of supply chains at this point, but what exactly does that mean for a PHP project, let alone a Drupal project? This talk will provide concrete advice, as well as a deep dive into how Drupal AutoUpdates are being built with security first in mind.

items.summary: The Future of SEO: Embracing Change and Innovation
items.description: In an era where digital landscapes are ever-changing, SEO has transformed from a mere keyword-centric practice into a sophisticated, holistic strategy. This talk will explore how the digital marketing realm demands adaptable and forward-thinking approaches to remain relevant and effective.

items.summary: What Do Marketers Really Want? Unpacking the User Research for Drupal CMS
items.description: Drupal CMS aims to simplify website creation for marketers and content teams. But what exactly do these users want, and how can Drupal stand out when many other CMSs promise ease of use?

items.summary: Coffee Break in Expo Hall
Creating Opportunities: From Internships to Drupal Careers
items.description: The Drupal community has long championed the values of diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity, but how can we ensure those values are reflected in the way we build and grow our teams?

items.summary: Drupal Certified Partners Round Table
items.description: Engage with the Drupal Association's Development and Fundraising team.

items.summary: Drupal Commerce's Starshot Roadmap
items.description: The vision for Drupal CMS (née Starshot) is bold and inspiring, inviting everyone in the Drupal community to consider how they might help end users accomplish even more with Drupal.

items.summary: End-to-end collaboration in Drupal with EditTogether
items.description: Using open-source tools, the EditTogether module provides secure, real-time collaboration inside the Drupal editorial interface. Editors may update content simultaneously and leave threaded comments requesting clarifications or changes. And it all works with content moderation workflows!

items.summary: Following Drupal core development: Is it possible to understand every added change?
items.description: Drupal moves faster than perceived. Keeping up to date is important and it may be challenging if you are not doing drupal core development full time. Let us explore Drupal core pace of changes, resources to follow it, and some experiences trying to do that.

items.summary: From Community Initiative to a Drupal CMS Track: the Dashboard Module
items.description: A couple of years ago we started defining a community initiative for getting a Dashboard system in core. We aimed to transform the user experience by providing a centralized interface for quick access to essential tools and key information.

items.summary: From Idea to Publish: Building a Custom GPT to Power Your Content Pipeline
items.description: ChatGPT is no longer just a buzzword—it’s now a powerful, practical tool that can dramatically enhance content creation when optimized effectively. In this session, we’ll break down how to build a custom GPT to meet your organization’s unique content needs.

items.summary: KEYNOTE: Drupal CMS Spotlights
items.description: Meet key Drupal CMS leaders to get insights on how the platform is being built and learn about future plans. Topics include Experience Builder, AI, user experience, documentation and more.

items.summary: Maximum efficiency by embracing WebOps Mindset
items.description: In today's fast-paced digital world, efficient website management is crucial for success. WebOps plays a vital role in streamlining processes to drive maximum efficiency.

items.summary: Planning a Takeover: A Success Story in Implementing Storybook, Drupal 10, and Layout Builder
items.description: Join in learning how the MIT Sloan Web Development team planned and executed a "Homepage Takeover"—a new content type, collection of components and editorial workflow, for our Drupal 10 website—in just 12 weeks.

items.summary: WordPress to Drupal:A Migration Survival Guide
items.description: Migrating from WordPress to Drupal CMS can feel like a big leap—especially if you’re used to WordPress’s page builders and want to maintain editorial flexibility.

items.summary: EXPO HALL NETWORKING
Beat the Gatekeepers: Build Direct Audience Relationships Through Content, Analytics, and AI
items.description: Discover how leading media brands use content analytics data to increase direct traffic, improve engagement, and boost content recirculation. Learn how to combine performance data with AI to turn passive readers into active participants.

items.summary: Distributions are dead, long live distributions: a Drupal CMS story
items.description: Drupal CMS is one of the most ambitious projects ever undertaken by the Drupal community. It tries to solve some of the same problems long faced by distributions…but it’s not a distribution. It’s built entirely on Drupal’s game-changing new recipe system.

items.summary: Driving today’s CMS with tomorrow’s artificial intelligence
items.description: It’s interesting to see the existence of an unrealistic realm in a realistic digital sphere with Artificial Intelligence and how probable it can be in Drupal’s world ?

items.summary: Drupal.org Engineering Panel
items.description: Join the Drupal.org engineering team for updates on our work to support the community.

items.summary: Honey, I Shrunk The Marketing Budget: How To Keep Improving Your Website In a Challenging Economy
items.description: Budgets for many marketing and web teams have been shrinking over the past few years. At the same time, the need to show results continues to grow.

items.summary: Jumpstart Your Drupal Projects with Recipes: Simplifying Configurations and Speeding Up Go-to-Market
items.description: Forum One initially created a set of modules and an install profile that we could use internally to get things like an accordion Paragraph and a simple WYSIWYG paragraph, reasonable security defaults, and more to have the go-to-market time for sensible default in Drupal be mostly completed.

items.summary: Launch your design system into hyperdrive with Starshot’s Experience Builder
items.description: Slides: https://bit.ly/kp-xb-ds-atlanta2025

items.summary: Mastering Drupal’s Core Site Building Features: The Keys to Flexible Content Management
items.description: Drupal offers a comprehensive suite of site-building tools that enable you to create dynamic, content-rich websites with little to no custom coding.

items.summary: Scaling quality mental health support in low income settings with two UN agencies
items.description: EQUIP (Ensuring Quality in Psychological Support) is a World Health Organization & UNICEF project using Drupal to deliver mental health support training and assessments in low and middle-income settings with low connectivity.

items.summary: The Best (and Worst) Themes, Modules, Widgets, Extensions, and AI tools for ADA Compliance
items.description: Are you looking for an easy way to remediate your site's accessibility issues? Are you looking for an 'out of the box' solution for Drupal accessibility, like a fully WCAG compliant contrib theme or an 'all-in-one' magic accessibility module?

items.summary: What the WordPress Conflict Means for Open Source Businesses
items.description: The recent dispute between WordPress founder Matt Mullenweg and hosting company WP Engine has sparked widespread discussion about the social and philosophical responsibilities of open source software.

items.summary: General Contribution
items.description: Generously Sponsored by ThirdAndGrove

items.summary: Mentored Contribution
items.description: If you are already familiar with the tools the Drupal community uses (such as the issue queue and/or a local development environment), but could still use help choosing issues and navigating the process, Mentored Contribution is for you.

items.summary: New Member + Local Drupal Community Meeting Space
items.description: Meeting space for organized local camps, associations, or communities with designated ambassadors. Creating a welcoming and functional space for fostering a sense of community and collaboration.

items.summary: Quiet Room
items.location: Location Executive Conference Room 219 - Hotel 2nd Floor
items.description: This room is available for mediation and reflection. It is in Executive Conference Room 219, located on the 2nd Floor of the Hyatt Hotel.

items.summary: REGISTRATION
First-Time Contributor Workshop
items.description: Do you want to contribute but are not sure how? Check out the First Time Contributor Pathway for a list of recommended sessions at DrupalCon Atlanta to help you figure out how to use your particular skills to contribute to the Drupal project!

items.summary: Marketing Contribution
items.description: Whether you are a talented writer or a visual creator, there is a place for you. Rosie Gladden will lead a social media writing track, while Nick Koger will guide participants in designing graphics using the Drupal brand.

items.summary: Lunch (for those participating in Contribution Day)
Trivia Night
